Package com.ducksabervn.projects.ketchup.frontend

Class AdminMovieListUIController

java.lang.Object
com.ducksabervn.projects.ketchup.frontend.AdminMovieListUIController
All Implemented Interfaces:
javafx.fxml.Initializable
public class AdminMovieListUIController extends Object implements javafx.fxml.Initializable
Controller for the Admin Movie List screen (AdminMovieListUI.fxml).

Responsibilities mirror the original AdminMovieListUI Swing class:

  • Populate the movie TableView from MovieRepository on load.
  • Search movies via MovieRepository.searchMovie(java.lang.String).
  • Sort movies by 8 criteria using the same Comparator chain.
  • Open AdminMovieFormUIController in ADD or EDIT mode.
  • Delete the selected movie after confirmation.
  • Logout: confirm → save CSVs on background thread → open Login.
  • Window-close (X): save CSVs on background thread → Platform.exit().

Provides addMovieRow(Movie) and getInstance() as static helpers so that AdminMovieFormUIController can notify this screen after a successful ADD, mirroring the original AdminMovieListUI.addMovieRow(Movie).

  • Constructor Details

    • AdminMovieListUIController

      public AdminMovieListUIController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize(URL location, ResourceBundle resources)
      Called automatically by FXMLLoader after all @FXML fields are injected. Sets up table columns and populates the sort combo box. Username is set later by initialize(String) before the Stage is shown.
      Specified by:
      initialize in interface javafx.fxml.Initializable

    • initialize

      public static void initialize(String username)
      Static factory — loads AdminMovieListUI.fxml, passes the admin username, loads all movies into the table, and shows the Stage. Replaces the original AdminMovieListUI.initialize(String username).
      Parameters:
      username - the display name of the logged-in administrator

    • getInstance

      public static AdminMovieListUIController getInstance()
      Returns the current singleton controller instance. Mirrors original AdminMovieListUI.getAdminMovieListUI().
      Returns:
      the active AdminMovieListUIController

    • addMovieRow

      public static void addMovieRow(Movie m)
      Adds a newly created Movie to the live table. Called by AdminMovieFormUIController after a successful ADD. Mirrors original AdminMovieListUI.addMovieRow(Movie).
      Parameters:
      m - the newly created Movie to append to the table

    • getStage

      public javafx.stage.Stage getStage()
      Returns the Stage of this screen. Used by AdminMovieFormUIController as the dialog owner.
      Returns:
      the admin list Stage
